text: It would be no exaggeration to say that my journey to Scotland was the most risky trip of my life, yet I would not trade it for anything. I had read an entire encyclopaedia entry about the region, written in such illegible old handwriting that I nearly gave up; but a single scenic paragraph about its barren moors and ancient empire of clans persuaded me to move. I packed light: a diary, a bottle of cheap perfume, a complimentary guidebook the agency gave me gratis, and an adequate supply of food. No reader could call me studious, but I do my homework. The voyage began on a crowded ferry. The captain, a civil man with a strong jaw, gave us a brief and reassuring address. "There is no menace today," he said, though static crackled on the radio and a sudden squall made the deck rotate beneath us. A van full of crates had to be lashed down with a jack and rope so it would not slide into the ditch of the lower hold. I felt my impulse to panic, but I would not plead for an early return. As we sailed, the sea began to sparkle. A crane lifted cargo on the far pier; gulls dove for crisp scraps; and the spray on my skin was cold enough to dazzle the senses. A fellow passenger, an immigrant from a distant empire, told me with an incredulous smile that he had once nearly drowned when a ferry sank, and he showed me, without spite, the proof: a long scar where a stake had nearly stabbed him. His story did not frighten me; it only sharpened my zealous wish to see everything. He spoke of the necessity of courage, and of the capacity of the human heart to recover. When at last we landed, the porch of the little inn glowed warm against the marsh. A sheriff with a sarcastic wit checked our papers; a bureaucracy of forms had to be edited and signed, and I had to magnify the tiny print with a lens to read it. The price of a room was high, but the owner, eager to guarantee my comfort, gave me a free pat of butter and a slice of bread with my dinner, and promised the rate would not rush upward. That night, by candlelight, I wrote in my diary about the strange snout of a wild boar I had glimpsed in the brood of trees, about the cement walls of a ruined chapel, and about the contemplation that fills a traveler's mind at rest. I confess my golf clubs stayed in their bag the whole trip; there was simply too much to see. A nuisance of midges plagued the doorway, and the running stream nearby never ceased its music. The static of worry faded. I learned that to magnify a small life with bold travel is no false promise but a real necessity, and that even a barren land can dazzle a willing heart. When the fury of the weather finally calmed and a ghastly fog lifted, I smiled, made one last assessment of my route, and began the long journey home. Along the road, the journey hit a snag. Our guide, once a pupil of an old conqueror's tale, tried to reassure us and converse calmly, though his nasal voice carried an uncomfortable edge. He would not edit the truth or add fluff: the arbitrary bill for the ferry was a pitfall, a real detriment to efficiency. To persuade the crowd, he raised an outcry against waste, explaining how industrialized agriculture could distend budgets. The implication was clear, sharp as a stab; even a disabled cart on our flank felt the hardness of the climb. They agreed to release the funds, and added a small suffix of thanks to the contract.
translation: 毫不夸张地说，我前往苏格兰的旅程是我一生中最冒险的一次，然而我不会拿它换任何东西。我读过整整一条关于该地区的百科全书词条，字迹是那种古老到几乎难以辨认的笔迹，我差点放弃；但其中一段风景如画、描写其荒芜的沼地和古老氏族帝国的文字，说服了我动身。我轻装上路：一本日记、一瓶廉价香水、旅行社免费赠送的一本导游手册，以及充足的食物供应。没有读者会说我是个勤学的人，但我会做功课。这次航行从一艘拥挤的渡船开始。船长是个下巴坚毅、彬彬有礼的人，给我们做了简短而令人安心的讲话。“今天没有威胁，”他说，尽管收音机里噼啪作响的静电干扰，一阵突如其来的飑使甲板在我们脚下旋转。一辆装满板条箱的货车必须用千斤顶和绳索固定，以免它滑进下层货舱的沟里。我感到想惊慌的冲动，但我不会恳求提前返航。航行途中，大海开始闪耀。远处码头上一台起重机吊起货物；海鸥俯冲去抢酥脆的残渣；溅在我皮肤上的浪花冷得令人眩目。一位同船的旅客，来自遥远帝国的移民，带着难以置信的微笑告诉我，他曾在一艘渡船下沉时几乎淹死，并且毫无怨恨地给我看了证据：一道长长的伤疤，那里曾有一根木桩差点刺穿他。他的故事没有吓到我；只是让我热切想看遍一切的愿望更加强烈。他谈到勇气的必要，谈到人心恢复的能力。当我们终于登陆时，那间小客栈的门廊在沼泽映衬下散发着温暖的光。一位带着讽刺机智的警长检查了我们的证件；一堆官僚的表格需要修改和签字，我得用透镜放大那细小的字才能读。一间房的价格很高，但急于保证我舒适的老板，在晚餐时免费给了我一块黄油和一片面包，并承诺房价不会急速上涨。那晚，借着烛光，我在日记里写下：我在那片树林里瞥见的野猪奇怪的猪嘴、一座废弃小礼拜堂的水泥墙，以及旅人休息时心中充满的冥想。我承认我的高尔夫球杆整趟旅程都待在包里；要看的东西实在太多了。一群讨厌的蠓虫在门口作祟，附近流动的小溪从不停止它的乐声。忧虑的静电消散了。我懂得了：以大胆的旅行放大一段渺小人生，并非虚假的承诺，而是真实的必需，即使一片荒芜之地也能使一颗甘愿的心眩目。当天气的狂怒终于平息、一团苍白可怕的雾散去时，我微笑了，对路线做了最后一次评估，便踏上漫长的归途。沿着这条路，旅程遇到了障碍。我们的向导曾是一位老征服者故事的学生，他努力让我们安心，平静地交谈，尽管他带鼻音的嗓音透着一丝令人不适的意味。他不愿粉饰真相或添加废话：渡船那笔武断的账单是个陷阱，是对效率的真正损害。为了说服众人，他大声疾呼反对浪费，解释工业化农业如何让预算膨胀。其含义如刀刺般清晰；连我们侧翼一辆抛锚的残破推车也感受到攀爬的艰难。他们同意拨款，并在合同上加了一小段感谢的后缀。
